triboulet.quote: I bear a terrible responsibility before God and am ready to give him every minute an account, but as long as I live, I will act with conviction, as my conscience commands me.
triboulet.author: Pyotr Stolypin

canio.quote: However many holy words you read, however many you speak, what good will they do you if you do not act on upon them?
canio.author: Buddha

perkeo.quote: Be empty of worrying. Think of who created thought! Why do you stay in prison when the door is so wide open?
perkeo.author: Rumi

chicot.quote: Let gratitude be the pillow upon which you kneel to say your nightly prayer.
chicot.author: Maya Angelou

triboulet.quote: But I don't want comfort. I want God, I want poetry, I want real danger, I want freedom, I want goodness. I want sin.
triboulet.author: Aldous Huxley, Brave New World

canio.quote: O light! This is the cry of all the characters of ancient drama brought face to face with their fate. This last resort was ours, too, and I knew it now. In the middle of winter I at last discovered that there was in me an invincible summer.
canio.author: Albert Camus

perkeo.quote: Man is always prey to his truths. Once he has admitted them, he cannot free himself from them.
perkeo.author: Albert Camus, The Myth of Sisyphus

chicot.quote: You think your pain and your heartbreak are unprecedented in the history of the world, but then you read.
chicot.author: James Baldwin, 1963
